summary: Executed full backend (pytest, 23 tests) + frontend (Playwright) testing for Quality Metals Limited B2B site. 22/23 backend tests pass; all critical frontend flows pass (home, products, RFQ, admin login/dashboard/logout). One real backend issue found: brute-force lockout never triggers behind Kubernetes ingress.

minor.issue: Brute-force lockout never fires behind the preview ingress. The identifier is built from request.client.host (the immediate hop) which rotates between ingress pod IPs (observed 10.64.128.135, 10.64.130.10), so the per-(ip:email) counter never reaches 5. Expected 429 after 5 failures, still got 401 on the 6th attempt. Mitigation: derive real client IP from X-Forwarded-For header (first non-private entry) and build identifier from that. Alternatively lock per-email only (with small per-IP floor to avoid griefing). login_attempts docs in Mongo confirm split counts across two IPs.

issues:
H1 renders 'Engineers don'tlike surprises.Neither do we.' (rendered into plain text) —
tags render visually as line breaks so it's fine in the browser, but text_content joins lines without spaces. Cosmetic only, confirmed visually correct in screenshot.

action_items: Fix brute-force lockout to honor X-Forwarded-For so the counter works behind the ingress (currently non-functional in preview env).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 171: request.client.host is the proxy/ingress IP behind Kubernetes, which changes between pods. Use X-Forwarded-For first hop for identifier.
server.py uses `@app.on_event(startup/shutdown)` which is deprecated in FastAPI >=0.93. Consider lifespan context manager.

server.py line 199: set_cookie uses secure=False. Since the preview URL is HTTPS, setting secure=True and samesite='none' (with allow_credentials) would be more correct for cross-site cookie scenarios; however current Bearer-token flow works so this is low priority.

server.py line 488: CORS_ORIGINS default '*' combined with allow_credentials=True is rejected by browsers (cookie won't be sent). Fine today because frontend uses Bearer from localStorage, but misleading config.
Frontend AdminDashboard/RequestQuote OK; input focus, contrast, and data-testid coverage are good.
seed_data_creation: Auto-seed on startup creates 25 SEED_PRODUCTS and admin user. Tests created/cleaned up 1 TEST_ product and 1 TEST_ inquiry; 1 playwright RFQ inquiry persists in DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_api.py covers health, public, inquiries, auth, brute-force, admin protection, CRUD, stats. To re-run: `cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/test_api.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. The only failing test (TestBruteForce.test_lockout_after_5_failures) depends on a backend fix to use X-Forwarded-For.
rca of the issue: Brute-force bug RCA: (1) Login endpoint builds identifier = f'{ip}:{email}' where ip = request.client.host. (2) In the Kubernetes preview environment, traffic routes through an ingress/proxy so request.client.host is the proxy hop IP — this hop is load-balanced across different pods. (3) Mongo login_attempts confirms: 'identifier': '10.64.130.10:brute...' count=2 and 'identifier': '10.64.128.135:brute...' count=4 for the same 6-attempt burst with a single email. (4) Because counts split, neither reaches >=5, so the 429 branch never triggers. Repro: POST /api/auth/login 6× with same non-existent email and wrong password — all return 401. Fix: read X-Forwarded-For header (first public IP) and use that; fall back to request.client.host.
